source: claude composition v0 (2026-05-14), 3 parallel band agents, alphabet v2 frozen 2026-05-13
status: v0 — Lee to curate in batches (TOWNS / BUILDINGS / CONSTRUCTION)

1.rationale: autonomous regions, each with its own bounded territory, distributed across the world

2.rationale: town sizes distributed in a graded falloff from many small to few large

3.rationale: interlocking fingers of city and country crossing alternately

4.rationale: valley floor as protected funnel of fertile land between hills

5.rationale: country roads a mile apart enclosing square cells of farmland

6.rationale: self-contained towns set apart in open countryside, each ring-fenced by distance

7.rationale: countryside as shared strata of working land, public access overlaid on farms

8.rationale: many small subculture territories tessellating the city as a mosaic

9.rationale: workplaces dispersed throughout the city rather than concentrated

10.rationale: the city's magnetic downtown core with reach radiating to all

11.rationale: local transport area enclosed by a ring road that bounds it

12.rationale: city government decentralized into community-sized branches of local control

13.rationale: wide swath of land between subcultures as hard separating edge

14.rationale: neighborhood as identifiable bounded spatial unit

15.rationale: strong continuous boundary around the neighborhood to hold its character

16.rationale: transit modes converging on primary interchange nodes

17.rationale: high-speed road as ring encircling the local transport area

18.rationale: learning dispersed across the city, students moving between many sites

19.rationale: shops scattered to follow need, clustering where webs intersect

20.rationale: small buses moving fluidly point-to-point across dispersed demand

21.rationale: buildings held low to four parallel strata of habitation

22.rationale: parking broken up and dispersed rather than bunched in large lots

23.rationale: one-way roads running side by side in the same direction, no crossings

24.rationale: sacred site as protected anchor within a ring of legal protection

25.rationale: permeable edge between people and water, access without destruction

26.rationale: full cycle of ages held in steady alternation within each community

27.rationale: masculine and feminine threads crossing through every piece of the environment

28.rationale: density peak set off-center rather than at geometric middle

29.rationale: concentric rings of decreasing housing density around the nucleus

30.rationale: activity nodes pulling life into tight knots, spaced ~300 yards apart

31.rationale: promenade as central backbone linking activity nodes in a walkable sequence

32.rationale: shops gathered along a single pedestrian street rather than scattered

33.rationale: night activities huddled together so their light reinforces each other

34.rationale: interchange as the focal crossing of transit lines, attention to the meeting

35.rationale: different household types interleaved side by side within each cluster

36.rationale: homes graded along a public-to-quiet gradient by where they sit

37.rationale: 8-12 houses tightly grouped around shared common land

38.rationale: row houses as parallel teeth along pedestrian paths, deep narrow lots

39.rationale: stepped terraces of dwellings climbing in discrete level transitions

40.rationale: old-people dwellings in nested rings, sprinkled across every neighborhood

41.rationale: small workplace clusters nested inside a larger common courtyard

42.rationale: industrial ribbon running as a backbone that also separates communities

43.rationale: university dispersed into a marketplace of overlapping offerings

44.rationale: town hall as political anchor placed at the busiest intersection

45.rationale: community shop-spaces forming a necklace ring around the town hall

46.rationale: many small autonomous shops gathered under one market roof

47.rationale: small health center as community anchor focused on prevention, open in both directions

48.rationale: housing interpenetrated with shops, schools, and workplaces

49.rationale: local roads forming dead-end loops off main routes, no through traffic

50.rationale: T-junctions instead of crossings — roads meet by branching, not crossing

51.rationale: local road softened to mostly green with sparse paving stones for wheels

52.rationale: paths laid at right angles to roads, forming a second crossing network

53.rationale: main gateway as deliberate break in boundary marking entry to a precinct

54.rationale: knuckle narrows the road where path crosses, slowing cars at the meeting

55.rationale: pedestrian walk raised one step above the road running alongside it

56.rationale: bike paths run alongside roads but held distinct by surface and edge

57.rationale: extra-safe children's path threaded through the city, sheltered from cars

58.rationale: carnival as a wild concentrated zone of dreams set apart in the town

59.rationale: quiet back as permeable refuge behind the busy front of buildings

60.rationale: public greens uniformly sprinkled, one within three minutes of every house

61.rationale: small enclosed square sized tight enough to feel inhabited

62.rationale: high places as vertical anchors rising above the urban field

63.rationale: raised bandstand as platform drawing dancing and crowd around it

64.rationale: natural streams running through the city as continuous water-paths

65.rationale: birth place as small anchor where family and community pass through

66.rationale: concentric sacred precincts, each entered through a deeper gateway

67.rationale: common land embedded among the houses that share it

68.rationale: swath of safe play-land connecting at least 64 households without crossing traffic

69.rationale: outdoor room shaped to hold people facing each other for hours

70.rationale: grave sites embedded as small pieces in parks, paths, gardens — never massed

71.rationale: still pool as quiet water-anchor in every neighborhood

72.rationale: sports places sprinkled around, action made visible to passers-by

73.rationale: loose pile of raw materials for children to assemble their own play

74.rationale: fenced common animal-ground gathering grazing creatures together

75.rationale: 8-12 people drawn together into one communal household

76.rationale: parents' realm, children's realm, and shared common — three branches off a center

77.rationale: couple's shared realm with each partner's private world embedded in it

78.rationale: one-room cottage core ringed by small alcoves

79.rationale: every household its own bounded home, owned and controlled

80.rationale: small autonomous workgroups of 5-20, each branching off its own way

81.rationale: public services broken into small autonomous departments rather than one mass

82.rationale: office departments placed close enough that they touch and people move between

83.rationale: master and apprentice rotating around the same work as shared hinge

84.rationale: teenage society as a small society embedded inside the adult one

85.rationale: tiny schools embedded in shopfronts one at a time across the city

86.rationale: children's home as second-home anchor in every neighborhood

87.rationale: many small shops each its own bounded territory, dispersed across the city

88.rationale: cafe front opening to the busy path, people sitting in view of the world

89.rationale: corner grocery placed every few hundred yards, one near each neighborhood's heart

90.rationale: single big hall holding hundreds of people criss-crossing among activities

91.rationale: inn rooms gathered around a shared common where travelers meet

92.rationale: bus stop as small public anchor at the gateway into a neighborhood

93.rationale: food stand placed where cars and paths meet

94.rationale: benches and sheltered corners sprinkled where people can fall asleep in public

95.rationale: Building broken into a cluster of smaller parts that nest social facts inside the whole.

96.rationale: Stories as parallel strata stacked vertically — height as count of layers.

97.rationale: Parking tucked behind a screening edge so cars are absorbed into building mass.

98.rationale: Sequence of progressively smaller realms, each entered through its own gateway.

99.rationale: One dominant building as the head, with smaller ones gathered as halo around it.

100.rationale: Pedestrian axis with rhythmic entrances rungs along its length.

101.rationale: Indoor street as enclosed axis cutting through the building as a shortcut.

102.rationale: Family of entrances grouped so all gateways read together at a glance.

103.rationale: Many tiny lots scattered, each one screened by walls, hedges, trees.

104.rationale: Building tucked into the worst patch of land; the precious scattered features around it are spared.

105.rationale: Building north, outdoor space south — paired so sun gradient lands on the open side.

106.rationale: Every outdoor space made positive by being given its own enclosure.

107.rationale: Building broken into narrow daylit wings fanning out from a trunk.

108.rationale: Buildings touch each other rather than stand isolated.

109.rationale: Rooms strung out along a long axis so each gets maximum separation.

110.rationale: Main entrance is the marked gap in the building's boundary, visible from approach.

111.rationale: Garden tucked half-hidden alongside the house, semi-permeable from the street.

112.rationale: Transition space at the door registers the change of light, sound, view.

113.rationale: Shortest path from parked car to house touches kitchen and living room directly.

114.rationale: Each outdoor space contains a smaller protected one looking into it.

115.rationale: Courtyard with view out and multiple doors in — enclosed but porous to life.

116.rationale: Roofs step down toward the ends of wings in a discrete cascade.

117.rationale: Sheltering roof tapers down to low eaves, wrapping the building like a hood.

118.rationale: Garden embedded in the top stratum — roof as inhabited terrace.

119.rationale: Arcade runs alongside the building, half-inside half-outside — permeable parallel edge.

120.rationale: Goals placed first as points, paths drawn between them afterward.

121.rationale: Path bulges in the middle, narrows at ends — an enclosure along an axis.

122.rationale: Building front sits flush against the street rather than set back.

123.rationale: Pedestrian density tuned so people read as a tight grouping, not scatter.

124.rationale: Pockets of activity ring the edge of a public square, animating its perimeter.

125.rationale: Stepped seats at a level change make a vantage point above the action.

126.rationale: Something — fountain, tree, statue — anchored roughly in the middle of the square.

127.rationale: Public-to-private gradient runs from entrance to deepest room along one axis.

128.rationale: Important rooms along the south edge — sunlight as orienting gradient.

129.rationale: Common area at the center of gravity, with circulation paths tangent to it.

130.rationale: A room at the entrance — enclosed pause between outside and inside.

131.rationale: Common rooms chained in a loop so movement flows through them, not past them.

132.rationale: Passages kept short so they read as hard edges, not corridors.

133.rationale: Staircase as a room with its own volume — vertical spine treated as a stage.

134.rationale: View glimpsed only through a narrow opening at a place of transition.

135.rationale: Light and dark alternating so people are pulled toward the bright places.

136.rationale: Private couple's zone embedded inside the house, distinct from common areas.

137.rationale: Children's beds clustered together, tucked toward the back of the house.

138.rationale: Sleeping rooms oriented to catch the eastern angle of morning sun.

139.rationale: Kitchen brought to the center of common life, gathering family around it.

140.rationale: Terrace as a permeable lip between house and street — neither fully open nor closed.

141.rationale: Each person gets a small room of their own embedded in the household.

142.rationale: Sitting spaces strung along the intimacy gradient, each more enclosed than the last.

143.rationale: Bed alcoves clustered around a common playspace — private nooks off a shared room.

144.rationale: Bathing room concentrated in one tiled zone, embedded between private and common.

145.rationale: Bulk storage as a closed contained zone set apart from active rooms.

146.rationale: Free-standing columns scatter through open wings, defining half-private cells.

147.rationale: Common table as a focus with people gathered in a halo around it.

148.rationale: Small work groups as clusters, each visible to itself but branched from the whole.

149.rationale: Welcoming items face each other across the entry; reception desk off to one side.

150.rationale: Waiting fused with another activity — two zones interpenetrating so waiting becomes positive.

151.rationale: Small meeting rooms scattered evenly through the workplace, not concentrated.

152.rationale: Workroom half-open to its neighbors — permeable edge instead of closed wall.

153.rationale: Rentable suite embedded in the house with its own private opening to outside.

154.rationale: Teenager's cottage attached at a single tangent point — independent yet adjoined.

155.rationale: Old-age cottages scattered through the neighborhood, each touching a larger household.

156.rationale: Working life and home life interpenetrate — workplace and dwelling as overlapping zones.

157.rationale: Home workshop set inside the dwelling but with its own marked edge to street.

158.rationale: Outdoor stair as vertical spine linking upper stories directly to the street.

159.rationale: Windows on two opposing sides of every room — paired light sources.

160.rationale: Building edge crenelated as a thick inhabited zone, not a thin line.

161.rationale: Sunny pocket where building wall and outdoors face each other, anchored by a seat.

162.rationale: North face stepped down so the sun reaches the ground beside the building.

163.rationale: Outdoor room defined by columns, partial roof, walls — full enclosure under sky.

164.rationale: Window seat embedded in the wall facing the street — eyes on the street, body inside.

165.rationale: Wall opens fully to the street — boundary becomes gateway.

166.rationale: Balconies, niches, galleries ring the building as a permeable outer skin.

167.rationale: Balcony recessed into the building's edge — permeable pocket inside the wall, not bolted on.

168.rationale: Floors meet the earth at a continuous touching edge, not perched above it.

169.rationale: Slope cut into discrete level transitions along contour lines.

170.rationale: Small orchards scattered on common land along paths and streets.

171.rationale: Trees placed as anchors that gather space around them — single spreading focus.

172.rationale: Plants intermingled in a wild weave, no formal beds or bare earth between.

173.rationale: Garden ringed by a closed edge that blocks noise from outside.

174.rationale: Trellis encloses the path overhead — corridor of green along the walk.

175.rationale: Greenhouse embedded in the house yet permeable to the garden — a glass room between.

176.rationale: A single quiet seat in a small enclosed pocket of the garden.

177.rationale: Vegetable plot fenced off as its own sunlit zone within the larger land — a dedicated patch.

178.rationale: Toilet and garbage flows funnel down to a single composting chamber.

179.rationale: Small alcoves embedded in the edge of a common room — pockets inside the larger volume.

180.rationale: Window place — a small inhabitable pocket built into the wall around the light.

181.rationale: Fire as the radiant center, social spaces gathering in a halo around it.

182.rationale: Heavy table with a pool of light over it, enclosed by surrounding walls.

183.rationale: Workspace enclosure tuned to roughly two-thirds wall — permeable edge between open and closed.

184.rationale: Stove, sink, counter clustered so cook can reach all three without crossing the room.

185.rationale: Chairs arranged as a circle, room itself helping to suggest the ring.

186.rationale: Beds drawn together into one shared sleeping cluster rather than separate rooms.

187.rationale: Marriage bed as anchored center, slightly enclosed with low ceiling or canopy.

188.rationale: Bed alcove embedded off a larger room — sleeping as a private nook, not a whole room.

189.rationale: Dressing room embedded as a transition zone between bed and bathing.

190.rationale: Ceiling heights stepped between adjacent rooms to register intimacy difference.

191.rationale: Indoor shapes broken from rigid rectangles toward irregular human-fitted forms.

192.rationale: Window opening looks out onto life beyond — a permeable break in the wall.

193.rationale: Half-open wall — neither fully closed room nor fully flowing space.

194.rationale: Interior window — a gap between two indoor zones rather than indoor and outdoor.

195.rationale: Staircase volume — pattern stub with no problem or solution text in source.

196.rationale: Doors placed at the corner — gap angled off the room edge, not centered on a wall.

197.rationale: Walls thickened to hold usable volume — inhabitable embedded space within the boundary.

198.rationale: Closets thickening the partition between rooms — storage as wall volume.

199.rationale: Counter pushed to the sunny edge — light gradient runs onto the work surface.

200.rationale: Narrow shelves layered across the wall, one item deep, nothing hidden behind.

201.rationale: A single shelf strip runs around the room at waist height — a continuous horizontal band.

202.rationale: Seat built into the structure — embedded in wall, window, stair, not freestanding.

203.rationale: Tiny cave-like spaces tucked under stairs and counters for children to climb into.

204.rationale: Hidden compartment concealed inside the fabric of the house — embedded behind a wall.

205.rationale: Structural shell wrapped around social cells — engineering nested into the human spaces it serves.

206.rationale: Whole building as one compressive body of clustered vaulted cells nested in continuous material.

207.rationale: Material ethics rather than a spatial gesture — no clean architectural primitive.

208.rationale: Globally complete and flimsy, then gradually stiffened — a density gradient woven through time.

209.rationale: Hierarchy of roofs: largest spans over largest groups, smaller branches stepping down in scale.

210.rationale: Floor and ceiling as paired horizontal strata — load-bearing layers stacked through the building.

211.rationale: Seats, closets, niches embedded into the wall's depth — alcoves carved into the perimeter mass.

212.rationale: A column dotted at every corner of every room — points marking the enclosure's geometry.

213.rationale: Column spacing tightens floor by floor going up — vertical density gradient in the stiffener comb.

214.rationale: Foundation continues the structure downward like tree roots fanning into the ground.

215.rationale: Raised slab on a low perimeter wall — a thin floor stratum held above grade by its edge.

216.rationale: Stiff tubular skin around a compressive core — a hollow column with its strength embedded.

217.rationale: Continuous beam ringing the room at its upper edge, tying columns and resisting vault thrust.

218.rationale: Wall as paired inner and outer membranes spanning between columns and frames.

219.rationale: Elliptical vault rises shallowly between supports — a tapered shell forming the floor-ceiling stratum.

220.rationale: Barrel or pitched vault with undulations along its length — a curved shell ribbed for stiffness.

221.rationale: Each opening sized to its place — a scatter of unique gaps rather than a standardized run.

222.rationale: A low sill drops the eye-to-outdoors threshold — vertical contact gradient pulled near the floor.

223.rationale: Splayed window reveal widens inward — a tapered opening softening the bright-to-dim gradient.

224.rationale: A low door makes passage deliberate — the gap dramatized as a felt threshold.

225.rationale: Frame as a thickened reinforcement ringing each opening — embedded into the wall fabric itself.

226.rationale: A fat column with a seat or step around it — single anchor haloed by inhabited margin.

227.rationale: Column meets beam at the corner — a filled hinge where two arms touch and transfer load.

228.rationale: Stair built over a vaulted void — stepping treads carried on a tapered compressive shell.

229.rationale: Vertical service chase with horizontal ducts branching into each room's vault edge.

230.rationale: A radiative source casting heat outward in all directions — a glowing center with a warm halo.

231.rationale: Stand-up window cut into the roof slope — an alcove-gap embedded in the sheltering plane.

232.rationale: Ornament caps the apex of the roof — a tapered marker pointing the top.

233.rationale: Hard public floors grading to soft intimate ones — a material gradient with a felt threshold between.

234.rationale: Shingles and boards lapped against the weather — overlapping A/B courses shedding water down the wall.

235.rationale: Inside surfaces soft and yielding to touch — a membrane that absorbs rather than reflects.

236.rationale: Wide-opening sashes admit breeze and scent — gaps positioned to catch and carry air through.

237.rationale: Solid door with a glazed upper half — opaque mass perforated by a transparent gap.

238.rationale: Tracery breaks daylight into a woven tapestry of light and dark across the silhouette.

239.rationale: Window divided into many small panes — a grid of mullions laddered across the opening.

240.rationale: A half-inch strip of trim wherever two materials meet — every joint marked by an embedded edge.

241.rationale: Outdoor seats placed at the right found spots — a scatter of anchored sitting points across the site.

242.rationale: Bench set against the front wall — interior life touching the street at one threshold point.

243.rationale: Low wall as a sittable boundary — solid enough to mark, soft enough to cross visually.

244.rationale: Canvas roof or awning — a soft permeable membrane filtering sun, wind, and dew overhead.

245.rationale: Raised flower bed softening the edge of a path or building — a living boundary with a hard rim.

246.rationale: Climbing plants trained around openings — vines woven through doors, windows, and trellises.

247.rationale: Stones with cracks of grass between — paved field alternating hard and living strips.

248.rationale: Soft brick and tile wearing with use — paving that yields slowly to feet and time.

249.rationale: Ornament concentrated at edges, joints, frames — decoration embedded where surfaces transition.

250.rationale: Surface colors tuned to the available light to bathe rooms in a warm overall glow.

251.rationale: Never two identical chairs — variety itself as the pattern, a loose distribution of differences.

252.rationale: Low lamps grouped over chairs and tables — clustered bubbles of light scattered across the room.

253.rationale: Personal things gathered around you — a loose scatter of meaningful objects clustering by use.
